fixes: Implement bi-weekly 30-minute role-playing sessions for support agents using the Gong.io platform to practice handling returns. | Conduct a thorough review of our existing return policy guidelines, ensuring they are clear, concise, and easy to understand. | Invest in advanced analytics tools such as [Datadog](https://www.datadoghq.com/) to track customer feedback on product quality more effectively. | Introduce a new 'Product Quality Scorecard' dashboard for management, allowing them to easily monitor and address issues related to product quality. | Regularly conduct taste tests with customers to gather direct feedback on the quality of our food products.

Actionability is hampered by vague execution steps (e.g., 'conduct taste tests' without specifying frequency, participants, or feedback mechanism). Concreteness is limited—while tools like Gong.io and Datadog are named, their use is not tied to food quality. Feasibility is low: investing in new analytics tools and building a scorecard dashboard is not trivial, especially for a single product issue, and taste testing lacks operational detail.

review_text: I went here whilst staying at The Venetian hotel over New Year's Eve and honestly had a terrible experience. I was very excited to visit the bakery as I used to watch cake boss on TV and absolutely LOVED the show. I tried the lobster tail which is highly overrated, the cupcakes which were dry and a few other pastries that aren't even worth mentioning as I don't even remember anymore they left no impression on me. Definitely does not deserve to have its own tv show- there are much better bakeries out there.

fixes: Implement bi-weekly 30-minute role-playing sessions for support agents using the Gong.io platform to practice handling returns. | Provide comprehensive product information sheets on all products, including nutrition facts, preparation instructions, and key quality attributes. | Conduct regular taste tests with customers to gather feedback on food quality. | Establish a food safety committee to regularly review and improve food safety protocols. | Develop a loyalty program offering discounts or exclusive access to high-quality items for loyal customers.

Feasibility is 2: taste tests with customers and a food safety committee require significant coordination, budget, and time—high effort. The loyalty program is not directly tied to remediation of quality issues.

Use these insights to inform future pricing decisions and mitigate potential issues before they arise. | Incorporate Price Transparency into Website Design:** Ensure clear visibility of all product prices on your website, including any additional fees or charges associated with them. Utilize transparent pricing models like 'pay-as-you-go' options or subscription plans which provide greater clarity about costs upfront. This will help reduce confusion around billing processes and avoid unnecessary complaints regarding hidden fees or unclear terms. | Improve Return Policy Process:** Streamline the return policy process by simplifying steps involved, reducing processing times, and enhancing transparency throughout the journey. Offer flexible options for returning faulty goods without charging restocking fees or imposing strict time limits. By providing better customer service experiences during returns, you'll likely reduce complaints about high prices due to unsatisfactory products received initially. | Encourage Customer Feedback Mechanisms:** Integrate multiple channels for customers to share their opinions and concerns easily - email surveys after purchases, live chat functionality on your site, social media listening tools, etc. Actively engage with customers through these mediums to gather insights into what factors influence their satisfaction levels with pricing structures. Regularly review these inputs alongside business performance metrics to make informed adjustments accordingly.

review_text: I was so excited to go here, but that excitement soon turned to disappointment. We had never been to a Carlo's Bakery in person, and we are fans of Buddy, of course, so we had to go while in Vegas. We waited in a decently long line, which gave us time to look at all the things that were wrong with this place. The display cases were filthy and messy. Frosting, sprinkles and crumbs were everywhere. There were even some cupcakes that had fallen upside down that just laid there in the case looking oh so sad. Several of the trays were empty, one of those being cannolis. One of the main reasons we wanted to go to Carlo's was was to try one of their famous cannolis. Sadly, that didn't happen. Mind you this was in the middle of the day. It wasn't like we were there 10 minutes til close. On top of that the food that was in the case was not properly labeled. Many things had no labels at all. This can be dangerous for folks with food allergies and annoying to other people trying to communicate what they want to the workers. We ended up getting a lobster tail and a napoleon type thing (a layered cake. no sign, so who knows what it was!). The lobster tails tasted stale and like it had sat in a fridge for a week. The cake thing was just not good. The flavor was not good. I took one bite of each and wished I had spent that $20 on a slot machine instead of desserts that wal-mart can do better than. I really hope Buddy and his family get a handle on things and get their locations back in shape, because I don't think this is the quality they had in mind for their customers.
issue: Display cases were filthy and messy with frosting, sprinkles, crumbs, and fallen cupcakes
fixes: [ ] Improve training for support staff on product knowledge by conducting monthly quizzes and providing additional resources such as FAQs, tutorials, and videos. | [ ] Implement a weekly newsletter highlighting new products, updates, and tips to keep customers informed about what's available in the store. | [ ] Encourage regular cleaning schedules and inspections at all locations to maintain a consistent level of cleanliness across stores. | [ ] Establish clear guidelines for stocking and organizing displays to ensure they remain neat and organized at all times. | [ ] Provide ongoing feedback and recognition programs for employees who demonstrate exceptional attention to detail and commitment to maintaining high standards of cleanliness.
rationale: The recommendations are off-topic: the customer issue is about physical cleanliness of display cases, not staff product knowledge or communication. No concrete tools, systems, or metrics are named. Actions like 'improve training' and 'encourage regular cleaning' are vague and unactionable. 'Monthly quizzes' and 'newsletters' are irrelevant to the physical cleanliness issue. Feasibility is low due to lack of specificity and implied need for new systems.

review_text: I was so excited to go here, but that excitement soon turned to disappointment. We had never been to a Carlo's Bakery in person, and we are fans of Buddy, of course, so we had to go while in Vegas. We waited in a decently long line, which gave us time to look at all the things that were wrong with this place. The display cases were filthy and messy. Frosting, sprinkles and crumbs were everywhere. There were even some cupcakes that had fallen upside down that just laid there in the case looking oh so sad. Several of the trays were empty, one of those being cannolis. One of the main reasons we wanted to go to Carlo's was was to try one of their famous cannolis. Sadly, that didn't happen. Mind you this was in the middle of the day. It wasn't like we were there 10 minutes til close. On top of that the food that was in the case was not properly labeled. Many things had no labels at all. This can be dangerous for folks with food allergies and annoying to other people trying to communicate what they want to the workers. We ended up getting a lobster tail and a napoleon type thing (a layered cake. no sign, so who knows what it was!). The lobster tails tasted stale and like it had sat in a fridge for a week. The cake thing was just not good. The flavor was not good. I took one bite of each and wished I had spent that $20 on a slot machine instead of desserts that wal-mart can do better than. I really hope Buddy and his family get a handle on things and get their locations back in shape, because I don't think this is the quality they had in mind for their customers.
issue: Customers experienced a long wait in line before being served
fixes: Enhanced Training:** Implement weekly virtual training sessions on managing returns for new hires and existing staff using the GoToWebinar platform. | Return Process Streamlining:** Create a dedicated online portal for customers to submit their return requests, reducing call volume and improving response times. | Real-time Analytics Integration:** Integrate real-time analytics from our CRM system into the agent's dashboard to provide instant insights into customer history and order details. | Incentives for Prompt Responses:** Launch an internal campaign offering bonus pay or rewards to agents who respond within 24 hours of receiving a return request.
rationale: Relevance is 4: addresses service speed with clear focus on reducing wait times. Actionability is 3: steps are implied but lack specificity (e.g., 'implement' without timeline or ownership). Concreteness is 3: mentions GoToWebinar and CRM, but 'real-time analytics' and 'portal' lack measurable KPIs or system names. Feasibility is 2: launching a new portal and integrating real-time CRM analytics require significant development, cross-team coordination, and cost—far from trivial. The incentive program also requires budget approval and tracking systems not specified.

review_text: Place was horrible. First and foremost, we grabbed a number which was B89 and waited over 2 hours for our number to get called. One of the most disorganized companies I have ever seen. I understand things were busy but the way of doing business was horrible and we noticed a lot of people leaving purely because of the wait. Second, once we finally got our number called, I was informed the pie that I had ordered 4 hours earlier was not made. The manager came out and spoke to me and before even apologizing, blamed it on me because I had not pre-paid. She tried to explain how the process worked if I would have pre-paid for the pie but did not apologize or offer a solution outside of waiting another 40 minutes for my pie to be made. Absolute worst customer service I have ever seen from a "manager." On top of that, pre-paying for my pie was never even provided to me as an option which only made things worse. Would not recommend this to anyone unless you are looking to waste a few hours on the strip and if you don't want to get your order correct.
issue: Excessive wait time of over two hours before being called
fixes: Implement a new service level agreement (SLA) with a clear definition of "excessive wait time" (e.g., less than 15 minutes). Provide customers with regular updates on their ticket status during the waiting period. | Use advanced analytics tools such as Zendesk Insights or Intercom Analytics to identify potential bottlenecks in the service delivery process. Regularly monitor these analytics to ensure quick response times are maintained. | Develop a proactive escalation protocol to address cases where a customer is waiting beyond the agreed SLA. This could involve escalating the case to a senior agent or team leader who can provide faster resolution. | Conduct regular training sessions for support staff to improve their problem-solving skills and product knowledge. These sessions should focus on common issues encountered by customers and how to efficiently handle them. | Utilize chatbots or automated responses to answer routine queries quickly and free up human resources for more complex tasks. However, ensure that there's still a clear path for customers to escalate issues when needed.
rationale: Relevance is high (4) as all recommendations address wait time, but the SLA definition of 'less than 15 minutes' contradicts the customer’s actual issue (2+ hours), making it unrealistic. Actionability is 3: steps are described but lack precision (e.g., 'regular updates' without specifying frequency or tool). Concreteness is 3: mentions Zendesk Insights and Intercom, but 'advanced analytics tools' is vague. Feasibility is 3: training, chatbots, and escalation protocols require coordination and resources, not trivial or zero-cost.

review_text: Don't send your child in alone. The girl behind the counter told our grandson the machine was broken so his kid cup was filled only one-third of the way (about 5 tablespoons) and watery, although he still paid full price and tipped. When he returned to the car he was obviously disappointed so I went in to ask about it. The shift manager told me that's how they come now and then added, "Have a nice day." I guess rudeness and minion size portions is how they roll! As for us, we walked over to Culliver's where my grandson got a real size cup of ice cream with great customer service!
issue: Customer received a significantly smaller, watery portion of ice cream but was charged full price.
fixes: Implement bi-weekly 30-minute role-playing sessions for support agents using the [Gong.io](https://www.gong.io/) platform to practice handling returns. This will ensure consistent delivery of clear and accurate information about product quality and policy while also fostering a culture of empathy and understanding among team members. | Provide comprehensive training on return policies and procedures for all staff involved in processing returns, including warehouse workers and delivery personnel. Ensure they understand how to identify faulty products, assess their condition, and accurately document any defects during the return process. | Create a dedicated feedback channel where customers can report issues with product quality or service. Encourage open communication by emphasizing that their input is valuable for improving the overall experience and addressing specific concerns. Regularly review these reports and take appropriate actions based on the insights gained. | Conduct periodic audits of returned items to verify compliance with established standards regarding packaging integrity, shelf life, and freshness. These audits should be performed independently by different teams within the organization to maintain objectivity and prevent potential conflicts of interest. | Develop a proactive approach to managing inventory levels by closely monitoring sales trends, seasonality patterns, and customer preferences. Adjust stock quantities accordingly to avoid overstocking certain products that may spoil faster than others due to increased demand or changing consumer tastes.
rationale: Relevance is reduced because while the recommendations address food quality broadly, they miss the specific issue: a customer receiving a smaller, watery portion. Actionability and concreteness suffer due to vague implementation details (e.g., 'periodic audits' without specifying frequency or tool). Feasibility is low—Gong.io integration, independent audits across teams, and proactive inventory systems require significant investment and coordination, disqualifying them as 'trivial' or 'low effort'.
